steps: By the Universal Coefficient Theorem and our computation of $H_*(\mathbb{C}P^n)$: $H^{2k}(\mathbb{C}P^n) \cong \mathbb{Z}$ for $0 \leq k \leq n$, all other cohomology groups zero.
Let $\alpha \in H^2(\mathbb{C}P^n;\,\mathbb{Z})$ be a generator (the first Chern class of the tautological line bundle).

The cup product $\alpha^k \in H^{2k}(\mathbb{C}P^n)$ is a generator for each $0 \leq k \leq n$: this follows from the Leray–Hirsch theorem applied to the fibration $\mathbb{C}P^{n-1} \hookrightarrow \mathbb{C}P^n$, or from the fact that $H^*(\mathbb{C}P^n)$ is generated as a ring by $\alpha$.
No further relations: $\{1, \alpha, \alpha^2, \ldots, \alpha^n\}$ freely generate $H^*(\mathbb{C}P^n)$ as a $\mathbb{Z}$-module.
Hence $H^*(\mathbb{C}P^n;\,\mathbb{Z}) \cong \mathbb{Z}[\alpha]/(\alpha^{n+1})$, a truncated polynomial ring on a single degree-2 generator.
